title: Souroubea amazonica
taxonomy: Souroubea amazonica is classified within the family Marcgraviaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the clammy apricot or clusioid family. This family is part of the order Theales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Souroubea amazonica, along with other plants in the Marcgraviaceae family, is characterized by their unique inflorescences and epiphytic growth habits. The family Marcgraviaceae is native to the Americas, with the majority of species found in Central and South America. Souroubea amazonica's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the clusioid family.
